what_it_does: This file is a channel attachment metadata record that associates attachment att-0eaef905 with board item item-2ecd462a in the dragnet-development channel, recording only the attachment type and timestamp.
observations: The file consists entirely of YAML frontmatter with no body content.
It references a target using the URI scheme 'board://item-2ecd462a'.
It records an added timestamp in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset.
standouts: Empty content beyond metadata; the only substantive signal is the board:// target reference.
conventions: Stores channel attachments under a channel-memory directory structure.
concerns: The file contains no descriptive content about the attachment, so its purpose is opaque from the file alone.
The referenced board item cannot be verified from this file alone.
notes: A bare metadata stub linking an attachment ID to a board item in the dragnet-development channel, with no explanatory body.
questions: What content or artifact does this attachment represent?
What does the 'board_items' attachment_type imply about the relationship?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to do exactly what attachment metadata is meant to do—record an association and timestamp—but it provides no descriptive context.
intention_signals: It seems intended to create a durable, timestamped link between a channel attachment and a specific board item so the relationship can be retrieved or audited later.
contribution: It contributes a timestamped association between an attachment identifier and a board item target within the dragnet-development channel memory.
summary_for_embedding: A YAML-frontmatter channel attachment record in company/channel-memory/channel_attachments/att-0eaef905.md links attachment att-0eaef905 to board item board://item-2ecd462a in the dragnet-development channel with attachment_type board_items and an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: This file was created to record and preserve the relationship between a channel attachment and a specific board item for later lookup or audit.
